petitioner_background: The petitioner is a self-represented individual seeking classification as an employment-based immigrant under section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Act as an alien of extraordinary ability in the arts, specifically in Peking Opera performance. The petitioner has been present in the United States since January 8, 2000, and was previously the beneficiary of an approved P-3 nonimmigrant petition that authorized him to work as an artist/entertainer from October 2, 2003 to January 1, 2004 (SRC 03 213 50833, filed by Song Shan Culture Center Inc. of Lilburn, Georgia). The director determined the petitioner had not established the sustained national or international acclaim necessary to qualify for classification as an alien of extraordinary ability.
outcome_reasoning: The appeal was summarily dismissed because the petitioner failed to specifically address the reasons stated for denial or provide any additional evidence. The petitioner's appellate submission was unaccompanied by arguments or evidence addressing the pertinent regulatory criteria at 8 C.F.R. § 204.5(h)(3). Although the petitioner indicated that a brief and/or evidence would be submitted within 90 days of the July 22, 2004 filing, more than fourteen months later the AAO had received nothing further. Pursuant to 8 C.F.R. § 103.3(a)(1)(v), an appeal shall be summarily dismissed if the party concerned fails to identify specifically any erroneous conclusion of law or statement of fact for the appeal. The AAO also noted that despite being present in the U.S. since January 8, 2000, there was no evidence the petitioner had been involved in any Peking Opera work during the last five years, and astonishingly no evidence of performance work even during the authorized P-3 period.
legal_citations: Section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Immigration and Nationality Act, 8 U.S.C. § 1153(b)(1)(A)
fraud_or_procedural_issues: Procedural dismissal under 8 C.F.R. § 103.3(a)(1)(v) for failure to specifically identify any erroneous conclusion of law or statement of fact on appeal. The petitioner indicated a brief and/or evidence would be submitted within 90 days of the July 22, 2004 filing but failed to submit anything in the more than fourteen months that followed. The AAO also noted the petitioner submitted no evidence of performance work even during his authorized P-3 period.
